title: A, An, The
description: The three tiniest words in English that trip everyone up. A before consonants, AN before vowels, THE when both speakers know which one.
summary_pt_br: Artigos em inglês: A antes de consoante, AN antes de vogal, THE quando os dois falantes sabem qual. Diferente do português, português quase sempre usa 'o/a', inglês deixa muitos substantivos SEM artigo (plurais genéricos, incontáveis).

scenes.text_en:
Bia pointed: 'A board. AN apple. THE ocean.'
Mr. Kumar smiled. 'A before a consonant SOUND. AN before a vowel SOUND.
THE when WE BOTH know which one — THE ocean we can see from here.'
scenes.text_pt:
A Bia apontou: 'A board. AN apple. THE ocean.'
Mr. Kumar sorriu. 'A antes de som de consoante. AN antes de som de vogal.
THE quando OS DOIS sabem qual — THE ocean que a gente vê daqui.'

scenes.title: The three rules
scenes.body_en: A + consonant sound: a dog, a book, a university (SOUND, not letter — 'university' starts with /j/). AN + vowel sound: an apple, an egg, an hour (silent H). THE when both speakers know the specific one: 'THE sun' (only one), 'THE book on the table' (specific). NO ARTICLE with plural general nouns: 'I like DOGS' (not 'I like the dogs').
scenes.body_pt: A + som de consoante. AN + som de vogal. THE quando ambos sabem qual. SEM ARTIGO em plurais genéricos ('I like dogs' — gosto de cachorros em geral).

examples.en: a car / an elephant / an hour (silent h) / a university (y-sound)
examples.pt: Foca no SOM, não na letra.

examples.en: THE sun / THE Internet / THE same thing

exercise.question: Which is correct?
exercise.explanation: 'Hour' — silent H, starts with vowel SOUND /aʊ/ → AN.

exercise.question: Complete: 'I like ___ coffee in the morning.' (general, no article)
exercise.explanation: General preference, uncountable → no article. 'I like coffee.'

scenes.title: Ordering at the bakery (A / AN / THE)
scenes.pt_summary: Artigos A / AN / THE em pedidos.

turns.speaker: ai
turns.text: Good morning. What can I get you today?
turns.pt: Bom dia. O que posso servir hoje?

turns.speaker: user
turns.target: I would like a coffee and an apple, please.

turns.speaker: ai
turns.text: Would you like the big coffee or the small one?
turns.pt: O café grande ou o pequeno?

turns.speaker: user
turns.pt_hint: THE porque o outro já foi mencionado.

turns.speaker: user
turns.pt_hint: AN antes de orange (som de vogal).

turns.speaker: ai
turns.text: Is that for here or to go?
turns.pt: É para comer aqui ou levar?

turns.speaker: user
turns.target: To go, please. I am in a hurry.

scenes.title: Listening story — A, An, The
scenes.pt_summary: Ouça a situação com atenção. Depois, escreva em inglês o que você entendeu — resumo, detalhes, seus pensamentos. O professor vai ler e comentar.
scenes.prompt_en: Write in English what you heard. A short summary is fine. Mention who spoke, what they said, and what you think about the situation.
scenes.prompt_pt: Escreva em inglês o que você ouviu. Pode ser um resumo curto. Diga quem falou, o que disseram e o que você achou.

paragraphs.speaker: Narrator
paragraphs.en: In this story, we meet Baker and a student. The three tiniest words in English that trip everyone up. A before consonants, AN before vowels, THE when both speakers know which one. Listen carefully and think about what each person says.

paragraphs.speaker: Baker
paragraphs.en: Good morning. What can I get you today?

paragraphs.speaker: The student
paragraphs.en: I would like a coffee and an apple, please.

paragraphs.speaker: Baker
paragraphs.en: Would you like the big coffee or the small one?

paragraphs.speaker: Narrator
paragraphs.en: That is the end of our short scene with Baker. Think about what you heard and how the conversation felt.

scenes.title: Recap reading
scenes.passage_en: At Amazing School, the class just finished "A, An, The". The three tiniest words in English that trip everyone up. A before consonants, AN before vowels, THE when both speakers know which one. Mr. Kumar reminded the students that practice is the key — listen, read aloud, write your own examples. Every small step adds up. The ocean in Praia do Sol keeps moving, and so does your English.

check.question: According to Mr. Kumar, what is the key to learning?
check.explanation: Practice — listen, read aloud, write your own examples.
check.hint_pt_br: A chave é a prática: ouvir, ler em voz alta, escrever seus próprios exemplos.

scenes.title: Listen to Bia
scenes.audio_text_en: Hello! I'm Bia, from Praia do Sol. Today in class we studied "A, An, The". I liked it because it was clear and short. Tomorrow we will practice again. See you then!

check.question: What did Bia study today?
options: A grammar rule about the past
check.explanation: She says: "Today in class we studied 'A, An, The'".
check.hint_pt_br: Ela fala claramente o título: "A, An, The".

scenes.title: Go deeper — free resources
scenes.body_pt: Fontes gratuitas e confiáveis para você estudar mais. Salve os links e volte sempre.
